# 1\.  Background

Enterprise System Operations (ESO) in HKEX is a team responsible for performing system monitoring, perform system operations and response emergencies. ESO heavily relies on operation jobsheet to determine the *"when, what and how"* the works are required. The jobsheet also acts as a key control to ensure all steps are performed by operators in a timely and accurate manner. This is achieved by operators signing task completion, recording timestamps, maker-checker verification and team leader review, etc.

Currently ESO is using paper jobsheets and online MS Word documents as work tracking for daily operations. The update and control of jobsheets are done manually, this creates a potential risk of human errors, and it is also time consuming in preparing and reviewing jobsheets manually. The current jobsheet manual process inherits a number of shortfalls, including:

1. Human errors can still occur with double human mistakes;  
2. Timestamps, job status, screen output may not be as accurate as captured by programmatical means;  
3. Lack of alerts until checker or reviewer has identified the issues;  
4. Difficult to generate management data; and  
5. Consuming huge efforts.

HKEX is now deciding to transform the jobsheets into a digital platform (namely eJobsheet) in order to reduce manual work and eliminate potential risk of human mistakes address the above shortfalls and at the same time build a foundation for continuous automation opportunities.

# 2\.  eJobsheet Compositions

## 2.1.  Job

A job describes the exact work that requires operator to execute. It contains the detailed procedures and information an operator will need to refer to in order to complete a job.

       ![][image1]

A job definition contains at least the following information:

❖  The day/date the job should be executed. 

❖  The expected start time and the expected end time of the job. 

❖  The dependency (can be multiple) that serves as the prerequisite(s) of the job. 

❖  The action descriptions and the procedures that require an operator to do.

### 

### 2.1.1.  New Job

    ![][image2]

### 

### 2.1.2.  Job Dependencies

There are 3 types of job dependency: 

i.  **Time Dependency** A job can be started once a defined time is reached.

ii. **Job Dependency** A job can only be actioned after its pre-requisite job(s) is completed.

iii. **Human Dependency** A job needs to wait for email, phone or human confirmation before it can be started.

A job can have multiple dependencies, therefore the above 3 dependency types are not mutually exclusive.

## 2.1.3.  Job Handling

When an operator works on a job, there are 3 types of job status: 

i.  **Successfully Completed** All required steps and procedures are done by operator, result is as expected.

ii. **Executed but Failed** All required steps and procedures are done by operator, however result is not as         expected. Operator is required to state failure details, indicate if rerun or defer is necessary or not. Operator leader's approval is required if defer is needed. 

iii.  **Special Handling**

* Sometimes for special reason, a job maybe cancelled, this requires operator leader's approval, and state who requested to cancel the job and why.  
* Similar to job cancellation, a job maybe required to be deferred, operator leader's approval is required and state who requested to defer a job and reason for deferral.

### 2.2. Jobsheet

A jobsheet contains multiple jobs. It states all the required activities operators need to complete.

#### *2.2.1. Jobsheet Template*

An operator leader can create a jobsheet template of a frequently used jobsheet. e.g. *Every Monday to Friday with same set of jobs, then an operator leader can create a jobsheet template called weekday template*.

The jobsheet template aims at saving the time of selecting jobs and putting them into a jobsheet every time.

#### *2.2.2. Execution Copy*

Once the jobsheet template is approved, an operator can use the jobsheet template to generate (clone) an execution copy.

Amendment of add/remove individual job is allowed on the cloned execution copy in order to give flexibility on real situations when creating a jobsheet.

Approval from the operator leader is required to confirm the cloned execution copy before it can be used by operators.

![][image3]

![][image4]

### 2.3. Role-based Users

The eJobsheet platform should be able to integrate with HKEX existing Active Directory (AD) and allow Single Sign-on (SSO).

There are at least 3 user roles expected, the privileges of each role should be defined within the eJobsheet platform.

| Roles | Privilege |
| :---- | :---- |
| Manager | Administrator rights to maintain group members and his/her permission in eJobsheet platform. |
| Operator Leader | Create new job and pass to manager for approval. Prepare on jobsheet template and jobsheet execution copy. If there is a job failure and/or with operational handling not following the defined procedures stated in the job, operator leader has the responsibilities on review, judge and approve the special handling. |
| Operator | Job execution. Mark as completed, incomplete or failed. Add notes or comments upon job completion if necessary. |

## 3\. eJobsheet Platform Functions

### 3.1. Operator Check-in & Check-out

The eJobsheet platform should provide concurrency and real time status of the job being completed, in order to avoid a situation that multiple operators working on the same jobsheet, a check-in and check-out jobsheet concept should be implemented. Once an operator check-in a jobsheet, all other operators can only view the real time working progress of the jobsheet, but not marking status on that jobsheet.

When an operator off from his work, he/she should check-out on the working jobsheet so the next shift operator can take up the rest of the jobs in that jobsheet. In case an operator forget to check-out, an operator leader can force check-out an operator from a jobsheet.

3.2 Approval Requirement

With the role-based user setup, all changes and special handling should obtain upper-level approval, basic approval identifications as below:

| Activities |  | Manager | Operator Leader | Operator |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| 1\. | Job creation | ☑ | ☑ |  |
| 2\. | Job creation approval | ☑ | ☑ |  |
| 3\. | Job deletion | ☑ | ☑ |  |
| 4\. | Job deletion approval | ☑ | ☑ |  |
| 5\. | Job update | ☑ | ☑ |  |
| 6\. | Job update approval | ☑ | ☑ |  |
| 7\. | Jobsheet check-in & check-out |  | ☑ | ☑ |
| 8\. | Force check-out | ☑ | ☑ |  |
| 9\. | Perform job execution (maker) |  |  | ☑ |
| 10\. | Check job completion (checker) |  | ☑ |  |
| 11\. | State comments after job execution |  | ☑ | ☑ |
| 12\. | Mark job completion – successful |  |  | ☑ |
| 13\. | Mark job completion – failed |  |  | ☑ |
| 14\. | Job failure approval |  | ☑ |  |
| 15\. | Defer job |  | ☑ | ☑ |
| 16\. | Defer job approval | ☑ | ☑  | ☑ |
| 17\. | Cancel/skip job |  | ☑ | ☑ |
| 18\. | Cancel/skip job approval | ☑ | ☑ |  |
| 19\. | Job special handling |  |  | ☑ |
| 20\. | Job special handling approval |  | ☑ |  |
| 21\. | Create jobsheet template |  | ☑ |  |
| 22\. | New jobsheet template approval | ☑ |  |  |
| 23\. | Generation jobsheet execution copy |  | ☑ |  |
| 24\. | Execution copy approval | ☑ |  |  |
| 25\. | Update jobsheet template |  | ☑ |  |
| 26\. | Update jobsheet template approval | ☑ |  |  |
| 27\. | Delete jobsheet template |  | ☑ |  |
| 28\. | Delete jobsheet template approval | ☑ |  |  |

### 3.3. Job Update Impacting Jobsheet

When there is a need to update a job and that job is previously being allocated into jobsheet(s), user will need to make decision whether this updated job will be applied to all jobsheet(s), or just partial jobsheet(s).

#### *Two situations in consideration:*

1. If the job update should apply to all jobsheet, then user can directly edit the targeted job.  
2. If the job update only applies to some of the jobsheets, then a new job should be created. ![][image5]

![][image6]

3.4  Approaching Due Alert

In order to prevent job execution miss, the ejobsheet platform should be equipped with a function to alert the operators a job is "going to expire" before the expected end time. The exact amount of time before the expected end time to prompt this alert should be a configurable parameter when creating a job.

![][image7]

3.5  Overdue Alert

If a job is not marked with status (regardless successful, failed, deferred or special handling) when the expected end time is reached, it is considered as overdue. The ejobsheet platform should prompt alert to indicate there is overdue job so the operator can pay attention to.

![][image8]

3.6  Calendar

Since some jobs are related to markets of different locations which have different trading days than Hong Kong, therefore it would be easier for users to create jobsheet if the ejobsheet platform can indicate a calendar of different locations (including Hong Kong).

3.7  Logging & Audit Trial

*3.7.1.  Logging*

The ejobsheet platform should have a robust logging mechanism that tracks all user activities to address "when", "who" and "what" questions. These logs should be detailed, capturing actions such as time, user, data changes, system errors, and other critical events. The logs should be securely stored and accessible for troubleshooting and problem investigation purposes.

*3.7.2.  Audit Report Generation*

In order to fulfill the periodic auditing activities, the ejobsheet platform should provide a capability for completed jobsheet generation based on:

* Date and time range

* Market

* Application

The audit reports should be in a printable format (e.g. PDF or docx) so they can be passed to auditors without relying the ejobsheet platform to view the information.

4\.  Dashboard

The dashboard should provide the following information: i.  A real-time percentage of completed jobs vs total jobs in a day. ii. Statistics of failed jobs, deferred jobs and special handling. iii. Which operator is checking-in which jobsheet. iv. A page of showing "approaching due" and "overdue" jobs of the day.

A suggested visual design by applications as below:

![][image9]

![][image10]

5\.  Services and Maintenance

The implementation of this ejobsheet platform should at least include:

5.1.  Problem investigation, troubleshooting, bug fix and problem resolution

The Company should conduct basic code review and scan before submission to HKEX. No password should be hardcoded in any program or script.

For any reported bug, coding issue, logic problem by HKEX, the Company should record all issues and provide resolution schedule and timeline.

5.2.  Known Security Vulnerability Patching and Updates

For any known vulnerability, or when HKEX is reporting any vulnerability to the Company related to the ejobsheet platform. The Company has to study and provide security bug fix and assist in applying security patches. Normal review requirement is once half yearly.

5.3.  User Training and Documentation

The Company should provide knowledge transfer and training activities to HKEX staff in order to have adequate knowledges and skills to operate the eJobsheet platform. This includes at least 3 lessons of onsite trainings plus a video recording for HKEX staff review purpose.

The Company has to provide detail documentations regarding to this project, which includes but are not limited to the following:

1. Server setup, system parameters, account creation.

2. Software or packages installed, installation location and configuration.

3. Program code deployment location, setup and configuration. Description on each program and script.

4. eJobsheet platform system implementation and technical design specification

5. Operation guides \- support guide and end user guide

6. eJobsheet platform functional specifications

7. User acceptance test (UAT) plan

5.4.  Maintenance and Support

The Company has to provide guaranteed support hotline 09:00 – 18:00 (every day) for the first 3 months after the ejobsheet launch to production. In case the module or functions are launched in different stages, the guaranteed support should be counted in each released module or function basis.

For the bug or implementation issue found after the production deployment, the Company has to provide bug fix and resolution at no cost to HKEX.
